sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root סָבַב) subst. circuit, 1Ch 11:8. Hence מִסָּבִיב from a circuit, from every side, round about (πάντοθεν, rings von allen Seiten), Deu 12:10 Job 1:10 Eze 37:21. מִסָּבִיב לְ from round about any thing, rings von (einer Sache) weg, Num 16:24, 27 Num 16:27. In acc. סָבִיב adv. around, Gen 23:17 Exo 16:13 and doubled סָבִיב סָבִיב around about, Eze 40:5, seq.; סָבִיב לְ prep. around (any thing), e.g. סָבִיב לַמִּשְׁכָּן around the tent, Exo 40:33 Num 1:53.

Plur. m. סְבִיבִים-

(1) those who are around any one, i.e. neighbours, Jerem. 48:17 , 39 Jer 48:39.

(2) circumjacent places (les environs), neighbourhood. Jer 33:13, בִּסְבִיבֵי יְרוּשָׁלַםִ “in the neighbourhood of Jerusalem;” Psa 76:12 89:8 97:2 .

(3) with suff. prep. around (any one). Psa 50:3, סְבִיבָיו נִשְׂעֲרָה מְאֹד “it is very tempestuous around him;” Jer 46:14.

Pl. f. סְבִיבֹות-

(1) circuits (die Umläufe), circles, orbits, in which any one goes. Ecc 1:6, וְעַל סְבִיבֹותָיו שָׁב הָרוּחַ “and the wind returns upon its circuits,” begins its circuits again, again begins to go round.

(2) i.q. סְבִיבִים No. 2, circumjacent places (Umkreis), Jer 17:26 Num 22:4 Dan 9:16.

(3) in constr. st. it becomes a prep., around, about. Num 11:24, סְבִיבֹות הָאֹהֶל “about the tent;” Eze 6:5 Psa 79:3 with suff. סְבִיבֹותַי round me, etc.
